word: H3026 - יְגַר שַׂהֲדוּתָא - Yᵉgar Sahădûwthâʼ - yegar' sah-had-oo-thaw' - Jegar-Sahadutha

sections.paragraph:
Original: יגר שׂהדוּתא

Transliteration: yegar śahădûthâ'

Phonetic: yegar' sah-had-oo-thaw'

BDB Definition:

Jegar-sahadutha = "witness heap"
  1. the mound of stones raised as witness between Jacob and Laban, called by Jacob in Hebrew 'Galeed'

Origin: from a word derived from an unused root (meaning to gather) and a derivation of a root corresponding to H7717

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
